task_completion.description: Load the three narrowband FITS files into an image processing application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image processing application (e.g., Siril, GIMP, DS9, or similar) is open, and the files '502nmos.fits', '656nmos.fits', and '673nmos.fits' are visible in the workspace, layer panel, or open file tabs.

task_completion.description: Apply contrast stretching to make nebular structures clearly visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dialog boxes for histogram transformation, levels, curves, or auto-stretching are visible, OR the images in the workspace show clear, bright cloud-like nebular structures rather than mostly black linear data.

task_completion.description: Map the FITS files to the correct RGB channels (Hubble Palette).
task_completion.visual_evidence: An RGB composition, channel mixing, or color mapping dialog is visible showing '673nmos' assigned to the Red channel, '656nmos' assigned to the Green channel, and '502nmos' assigned to the Blue channel.

task_completion.description: Merge the channels into a single RGB color composite.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A single, merged color image of the nebula is visible in the main workspace, displaying the characteristic false-color Hubble Palette (typically featuring golden, cyan, and greenish hues).

task_completion.description: Export the final composite as a TIFF file with the correct name and location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog is visible showing the filename 'eagle_nebula_hubble_palette.tiff' being saved into the '~/AstroImages/eagle_nebula/' directory, or a file manager shows this newly created file in the correct folder.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical image processing application rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a GUI application (like Siril, GIMP, Photoshop, etc.) to process the images, rather than just executing ImageMagick or Python scripts in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent did not download a pre-made image of the Eagle Nebula from the internet.
integrity.visual_evidence: No web browser is open to search results or image pages for the Eagle Nebula. The composite must be built from the provided FITS files.

integrity.description: The agent actually performed the channel mapping and stretching steps.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show intermediate steps of image manipulation (layers, channel dialogs, histogram adjustments) rather than just renaming an existing file to the target filename.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires the standard Hubble Palette (SHO mapping): Red channel = 673nm (SII), Green channel = 656nm (H-alpha), and Blue channel = 502nm (OIII). The final image must be saved as 'eagle_nebula_hubble_palette.tiff' in the '~/AstroImages/eagle_nebula/' directory. The source files are real ESA Hubble FITS Liberator Datasets of the Eagle Nebula (M16-A).
